headline: Trump vows to send Iran 'back to the stone ages,' but offers no end to the war
description: The televised address by the US president on Wednesday was an exercise in self-congratulation over supposed American 'victories,' even as a major crisis threatens the global economy.

Nice's 2030 Winter Olympics projects challenged by political struggles
Sarkozy appeal trial hears moving testimonies from families of UTA flight bombing victims
description: On Wednesday, 13 relatives of victims killed in the 1989 plane bombing took to the witness stand to express their anger at former French president Nicolas Sarkozy. He is accused of making a pact with the Libyan masterminds of the bombing to receive campaign funding.

headline: Tax reassessments target wealthy French who pay no income tax
description: Over the past three years, tax authorities have audited between a quarter and a third of households with significant real estate assets that paid no income tax. Around 58% of them faced a reassessment, the Finance Ministry said on Wednesday, April 1.

headline: The mystery of the Green River's 'uphill flow' in the American West has been solved after 150 years
description: A young researcher from the University of Glasgow has offered an explanation for a geological anomaly that has puzzled scientists since the 19th century.

headline: The wild story of a new Molière play, co-written with AI
description: For nearly three years, the Molière Sorbonne Theater in Paris and a trio of artists have been working to produce a comedy, 'The Astrologer or the False Omens,' that the playwright might have written had he lived longer.

French archives of Spaniards who fled Franco's dictatorship are now available online
description: From March 30, over 180,000 records are accessible on the French Office for the Protection of Refugees and Stateless Persons' website, due to funding from Spain aimed at preserving the memory of people's 40 years of exile in France.

headline: The French cadets suspected of setting a boat ablaze in Japan
description: An investigation is underway into how four cadets from France's elite Saint-Cyr military academy came to be accused of setting a boat on fire while on leave in Okinawa in 2024. The incident has fueled local distrust of military personnel on the island.

headline: Europeans remain powerless to tackle Orban, seen as too close to the Kremlin
description: With Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, who has close ties with the Kremlin, vetoing new aid to Ukraine, European leaders hope their relations with Budapest will return to normal after Hungary's April 12 parliamentary elections.
byline: Jean-Baptiste Chastand,  Philippe Jacqué and  Virginie Malingre
